Life_Goal.Description: Create and sustain a tangible cultural-heritage project that preserves the history, techniques, and safety culture of stunt performance in Los Angeles and supports the next generation of performers. Over the next 5–10 years Jackson will: (1) produce a 30–60 minute documentary compiling 10–15 oral histories from veteran stunt performers and key archive materials (target completion: years 2–4); (2) launch an annual two-day "Stunt Heritage & Safety" workshop in Los Angeles that combines historical context, safety clinics, and mentorship, with an initial cohort of ~30 participants in year 3 and growth to 75+ by year 6; and (3) establish a dedicated $25,000 seed fund or donor-advised account (built from a mix of personal allocation, modest fundraising, and sponsorship) by year 5 to provide micro-grants or a small annual scholarship (e.g., $1,500–3,000/year) for training, equipment, or travel for emerging stunt performers. Jackson will fund this sustainably by allocating a defined portion of surplus income/savings (for example 5–10% of annual savings growth or an initial $15,000–30,000 commitment), seeking festival distribution and modest crowdfunding for the documentary, and tracking success with concrete metrics: documentary completion and festival/online distribution, number of workshop participants and instructor partners, number of oral histories archived (target 50+ resources by year 7), and the establishment and disbursement of the seed fund. This goal matters to Jackson because it honors the craft that shaped his career, creates a legacy that improves safety and professional recognition for stunt performers, and gives his creative, values-driven INFP instincts a structured, financially realistic way to invest his earnings into a meaningful, long-term project.
